chief_complaint: An 11-year-old spayed female Dogo Argentino dog with megaesophagus was managed with mechanical ventilation for aspiration pneumonia.
history: The dog had been treated previously with omeprazole and amoxicillin-clavulanic acid for 2.5 weeks. Airway cytology showed inflammation and numerous yeast organisms most consistent with Candida. Therapy with fluconazole was initiated, but the dog's condition deteriorated.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Airway cytology: inflammation and numerous yeast organisms most consistent with Candida. Airway culture and postmortem culture of lung tissue: Candida glabrata and polymicrobial infection.
